Scenario.overview: Transportation Systems Complex Brief summary of the attack: The Juicy Mix campaign targets transportation systems with a complex attack plan involving multiple stages, including reconnaissance, resource development, initial access, execution, persistence, privilege escalation, defense evasion, credential access, discovery, lateral movement, collection, exfiltration, and impact.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets Cloud Instance Target Artifact (Tasks) Target Artifact (Social media) OS Process Target Artifact (Application) Target Artifact (Boot) Application Log Target Artifact (Mechanisms) Dynamic Link Library File System Credential Store Network Traffic User Account File
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Email Artificial Intelligence Social Media Process Creation (Mechanism) Delivery Method Code Injection Memory Injection Scheduled Task/Job Bootkit Application Log Content (Mechanism) Abuse Elevation Control Mechanism SIP and Trust Provider Hijacking Command Execution (Mechanism) Windows Registry Key Access (Mechanism) Pass the Hash Reg.exe Utility Network Traffic Flow (Mechanism) User Account Authentication (Mechanism) File Access (Mechanism) File and Directory Discovery Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol Data Encrypted Network Traffic Content (Mechanism) File Modification (Mechanism)

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attacker uses artificial intelligence to gather information about transportation systems' networks and vulnerabilities.

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attacker identifies potential targets and their employees through social media and online platforms.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.capec: CAPEC-670: Software Development Tools Maliciously Altered
stage.description: The attacker creates a custom backdoor like Mango to bypass detection.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2024-43461 (Suggested): Microsoft Windows MSHTML Platform contains a user interface (UI) misrepresentation of critical information vulnerability that allows an attacker to spoof a web page.
stage.description: The attacker sends targeted phishing emails to transportation system employees with a link to the custom malware.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2021-21985 (Suggested): VMware vSphere Client contains an improper input validation vulnerability in the Virtual SAN Health Check plug-in, which is enabled by default in vCenter Server, which allows for remote code execution.
stage.description: The attacker compromises transportation system websites or third-party vendors' websites to infect visitors.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2022-1388 (Suggested): F5 BIG-IP contains a missing authentication in critical function vulnerability which can allow for remote code execution, creation or deletion of files, or disabling services.
stage.description: The attacker injects the custom malware into the compromised system.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2023-22527 (Suggested): Atlassian Confluence Data Center and Server contain an unauthenticated OGNL template injection vulnerability that can lead to remote code execution.
stage.capec: CAPEC-671: Requirements for ASIC Functionality Maliciously Altered
stage.description: The attacker bypasses detection by hiding the malicious code within legitimate processes.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: The attacker schedules tasks to maintain persistence on the compromised system.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: The attacker modifies the boot process to ensure persistence even after reboot.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: The attacker exploits vulnerabilities in public-facing applications to gain elevated privileges.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: The attacker uses existing elevation control mechanisms to escalate privileges.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.technique_name: T1198: SIP and Trust Provider Hijacking
stage.description: The attacker disables anti-virus software to evade detection.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.description: The attacker modifies system firmware to hide evidence of the attack.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.capec: CAPEC-226: Session Credential Falsification through Manipulation
stage.description: The attacker steals credentials from compromised accounts.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.capec: CAPEC-461: Web Services API Signature Forgery Leveraging Hash Function Extension Weakness
stage.description: The attacker uses stolen hashes to access other systems.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: The attacker scans the network for vulnerable systems.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: The attacker queries the registry for sensitive information.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.description: The attacker uses remote services to move laterally across the network.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.capec: CAPEC-645: Use of Captured Tickets (Pass The Ticket)
stage.description: The attacker uses valid accounts to move laterally.

stage.name: Collection
stage.description: The attacker stages collected data for exfiltration.

stage.name: Collection
stage.description: The attacker discovers files and directories containing sensitive information.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: The attacker exfiltrates data using alternative protocols.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: The attacker encrypts data before exfiltration.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attacker disrupts transportation systems to cause maximum impact.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC-675: Retrieve Data from Decommissioned Devices
stage.description: The attacker destroys data to cause long-term damage.

Scenario.summary: The Juicy Mix campaign targets transportation systems with a complex attack plan involving multiple stages, including reconnaissance, resource development, initial access, execution, persistence, privilege escalation, defense evasion, credential access, discovery, lateral movement, collection, exfiltration, and impact.
